title: Independent review
summary: Review MUST independently evaluate the workspace, remediate bounded non-structural defects, and route only major, structural, ambiguous, or unresolved hard findings back to implementation.
instructions: Review MUST verify acceptance criteria, test quality, maintainability, and scope control against evidence before choosing a verdict.

The reviewer MUST remediate a finding when intended behavior is unambiguous, the fix is local and low-risk, and it does not alter architecture, public interfaces, data or persistence models, security boundaries, dependency strategy, requirements, or the approved approach.
A finding MUST route to implementation when it requires a broad refactor, cross-component redesign, migration, structural decision, operator judgment, or has uncertain blast radius.

Each finding MUST include severity, evidence, remediation ownership, and whether the reviewer resolved it or left it unresolved. Reviewer edits MUST be disclosed and validated rather than applied silently.
The review gate MUST fail when any hard blocker remains unresolved; a hard finding repaired and validated during review does not require a review-to-implementation loop.

Harness governance, path-resolution, validator, renderer, or artifact-contract defects MUST be recorded as governance/artifact diagnostics for ship review and MUST NOT produce a review-to-implementation transition. Only legitimate unresolved implementation defects may route to the coder.

Before submission, review findings, acceptance-criterion coverage, verdict consistency, reviewer remediation, and the referenced review artifact MUST run their resolved policy-bound validators under VALID-001.
